title: Migrate from CRA to Vite
description:
Replace Create React App with Vite for faster dev server, better build tooling, and Keystatic compatibility.

Acceptance criteria:
- Remove react-scripts dependency
- Set up Vite with React plugin
- Migrate index.html to Vite root
- Update npm scripts (dev, build, deploy)
- Verify CSS Modules still work
- Verify fonts (Oswald, Raleway) still load correctly
- Existing functionality unchanged after migration

title: Set up Keystatic CMS for content management
description:
Replace hardcoded employment data in src/data/employment.js with Keystatic CMS.

Acceptance criteria:
- Install and configure Keystatic (keystatic.config.ts)
- Define a collection schema for employment entries (company, title, startYear, employmentYears, stack, summary, description, extendedDescription)
- Migrate existing employment data into Keystatic content files (markdown/yaml)
- Add a personal bio content singleton for the bio text currently hardcoded in Main.js
- Update components to read from Keystatic-generated content
- Ensure dev server works with Keystatic local mode
- Add extendedDescription field to support the tell-me-more feature

Depends on: rtm-001 (Keystatic needs Vite or Next.js, not CRA)

title: Redesign timeline as a scrubbable carousel
description:
Replace the current vertical dot timeline with a scrubbable carousel-style timeline.

Desktop: positioned to the left of job info (existing layout direction is fine, but the timeline UI itself should be reimagined as a carousel/slider that can be scrubbed through time).

Mobile: positioned across the top of the screen, horizontally scrollable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Timeline renders as a carousel/slider that can be scrubbed
- Desktop: timeline on the left, content on the right
- Mobile: timeline horizontal across the top
- Smooth scrolling/scrubbing between years
- Active year/job clearly highlighted
- Clicking or scrubbing to a year shows that job's details
- Touch-friendly on mobile (swipe to scrub)
- Maintain connection to employment data (from Keystatic after rtm-002)

title: Add tell-me-more / oh-that's-nice expandable job details
description:
Add an expandable details section to job entries with personality.

Behavior:
- Each job shows the main info (company, title, years, stack, summary) statically - always visible
- Below the main info, a button reads 'tell me more'
- Clicking it smoothly expands the extendedDescription into view
- The button text changes to 'oh, that's nice'
- Clicking again collapses the extended details
- The main info stays static on the page (no layout shift)

Acceptance criteria:
- Button toggles between 'tell me more' and 'oh, that's nice'
- Smooth expand/collapse animation
- Extended description content comes from Keystatic (extendedDescription field)
- No layout shift on the static content above
- Works well on both desktop and mobile

title: Make footer sticky to bottom of viewport
description:
The footer with social icons (email, LinkedIn, GitHub, Medium) should be sticky/fixed to the bottom of the viewport window at all times.

Acceptance criteria:
- Footer stays fixed to bottom of viewport
- Footer doesn't overlap content (add bottom padding to main content)
- Works on both desktop and mobile
- Icons remain accessible and properly spaced
- Footer is visually subtle but always accessible

title: Mobile optimization pass
description:
Ensure every component and layout is optimized for mobile viewports.

Acceptance criteria:
- Timeline carousel is horizontal and touch-scrollable on mobile
- Job info content is properly sized and readable
- Tell-me-more section works with smooth expand/collapse
- Sticky footer doesn't obscure content
- ThemePicker is accessible on mobile
- Profile photo and name are properly sized
- Tech stack badges wrap nicely
- No horizontal overflow on any viewport
- Test on common breakpoints (320px, 375px, 414px, 768px)

title: Refresh seasonal theme colors
description:
Tweak the seasonal color palettes slightly. Keep the same seasonal concept (winter, spring, summer, autumn + default) and the same CSS custom property system (--primary-color, --secondary-color, --text-color, --link-color).

Preserve the fonts (Oswald for headings, Raleway for body).

Acceptance criteria:
- All 5 themes (default, winter, spring, summer, autumn) have refreshed colors
- Colors are tweaked, not completely changed - maintain the seasonal feel
- Ensure sufficient contrast ratios for accessibility
- Gradient backgrounds still look good
- Link colors are distinguishable from text
- ThemePicker still works to switch between seasons

title: Position timeline adjacent to content on desktop
description:
Currently the timeline is fixed to the far left of the viewport (left: 40px) while the main content is centered in the window. This creates a large visual gap between the timeline and the content it controls.

The timeline should sit immediately to the left of the content area, so the two feel connected as a single unit rather than separate elements on opposite sides of the screen.

Acceptance criteria:
- Timeline is positioned directly to the left of the main content area on desktop
- Timeline and content feel visually connected (no large gap)
- Remove the fixed left: 40px positioning in favor of a layout-relative approach
- Remove the padding-left: 160px workaround on .app
- The combined timeline+content unit remains centered in the viewport
- Timeline still vertically centered relative to content
- Mobile layout unchanged (horizontal scrollable at top)

title: Replace timeline dots with tick marks (ruler style)
description:
Replace the circular dot indicators on the timeline with tick mark / ruler-style indicators. The timeline should look more like a ruler or measuring tape than a series of bubbles.

Acceptance criteria:
- Timeline nodes use horizontal tick marks instead of circles
- Active tick mark is visually distinct (bolder, longer, or highlighted)
- Year labels remain next to their tick marks
- Connecting line between ticks still present (the 'ruler spine')
- Consistent with the tick/ruler aesthetic on both desktop (vertical) and mobile (horizontal)
- Hover state on tick marks provides visual feedback

title: Make desktop timeline scrollable with limited visible entries
description:
On desktop, the timeline currently shows all job entries at once. With 7 entries the list is long and will only grow. Limit the visible window to 3-4 entries and make the timeline scrollable/scrubbable on desktop as well.

Acceptance criteria:
- Desktop timeline shows only 3-4 entries at a time in a scrollable container
- Scrolling/scrubbing through the timeline reveals additional entries
- Active entry stays visible (auto-scroll to active if needed)
- Smooth scroll behavior
- Mouse wheel scrolling works on the timeline area
- Click/drag scrubbing works on the timeline area

title: Add scroll overflow indicators to timeline carousel
description:
Since the timeline is scrollable on both mobile and desktop, add visual indicators that communicate there are more entries to see beyond the visible area.

Acceptance criteria:
- Fade/gradient overlay at the edges where more content exists (e.g. top/bottom fade on desktop, left/right fade on mobile)
- OR subtle arrow indicators pointing in the scroll direction
- Indicators only appear when there is actually more content in that direction
- Indicators disappear when scrolled to the end in that direction
- Works on both desktop (vertical scroll) and mobile (horizontal scroll)
- Non-intrusive - doesn't block interaction with timeline entries
